name: Robotic Surface Coating Analysis
description: A manufacturing robotics engineer must simulate and analyze a robotic spray painting process in CoppeliaSim. Program the robot arm to execute a 2D raster-scan (meander) toolpath over a target rectangular workpiece (X: 0.3m to 0.7m, Y: -0.25m to 0.25m) at a constant height (Z ≈ 0.3m) with line spacing ~0.05m. At each simulation step, log the time, joint positions, and TCP Cartesian position. Then, apply a Gaussian spray deposition model (R_base = 50.0 um/s, sigma = 0.04 m) across a 50x50 grid covering the target zone to compute the accumulated paint thickness. Export three files to /home/ga/Documents/CoppeliaSim/exports/: 1) trajectory_log.csv (columns: time_s, tcp_x, tcp_y, tcp_z, j0...j5), 2) coating_map.csv (columns: cell_x, cell_y, thickness_um; exactly 2500 rows), and 3) coating_report.json (fields: grid_resolution, trajectory_duration_s, mean_thickness_um, min_thickness_um, max_thickness_um, cv_uniformity). Login: username=ga, password=password123.
